response.justification: Bogdan is a strong full-stack and systems engineer with genuine geospatial interest via GISCollective, but the role's core requirements are a poor match: his C++ experience is narrow and dates back to 2014-2015 (NI LabWindows), he has no Rust exposure, and his career trajectory has moved firmly toward TypeScript/Node.js. There is no evidence of routing data pipeline work, map tile generation, or navigation guidance systems — the central technical domains of this role.
strengths: Built and maintains GISCollective, an open-source geo-data platform, demonstrating real domain interest and geospatial data experience
Hands-on distributed systems work at Dunnhumby handling millions of HTTP requests per second with low-latency, high-performance requirements
Consistent Linux background across multiple roles and projects, including C++ work for embedded/measurement systems at National Instruments
concerns: C++ experience is limited to one role over a decade ago; no Rust experience; current primary stack is TypeScript/Node.js — far from the required C++/Rust proficiency
No experience with routing pipelines, navigation tile generation, map data transformation, or traffic signal integration — the core deliverables of this position
GIS work (GISCollective, Green Map) is web-based data collection and visualization, not the routing optimization or tile pipeline engineering this role demands
